channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Deadly Israel shooting ruled a terror attack
item.description: A police officer was killed and four others were injured when a terrorist opened fire near a highway in Ashdod, Israel, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taliban-run media stops showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Taliban run-media have stopped showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces to comply with morality laws, an official confirmed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After 67 days adrift, a Russian man was rescued but his brother and nephew are dead
item.description: A Russian man has been rescued in the Sea of Okhotsk after surviving for two months in an inflatable boat that lost its engine, but his brother and nephew have died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Springfield rhetoric reverberates in Florida's Haitian community
item.description: Republican attacks on Haitian migrants in Ohio are reverberating in Florida, where Democrats are hoping to mobilize the country's largest Haitian American electorate to close the gap in their uphill battle for statewide races.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean roads in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a symbolic display of anger, North Korea on Tuesday blew up the northern section of unused roads that once linked it with the South, with the rivals exchanging threats days after the North claimed that its rival flew drones over its capital Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Violence-hit Pakistan locks down the capital for an Asian security meeting
item.description: Pakistani authorities have locked down the capital in a major security move as senior officials from several nations arrive for an Asian security group meeting.
item.dc:creator: Munir Ahmed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China unveils its plans to turn its feats in space exploration into scientific advances
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China has made great strides in exploring space in recent years, rocketing astronauts to its own space station and bringing back rocks from the moon. Now it wants to turn those feats into scientific advances.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Father called UK police to confess to killing daughter, 10, in England after he fled to Pakistan
item.description: The father of a 10-year-old girl who was found dead in England called U.K. police, after flying to Pakistan, to say that he had killed his daughter, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: Hong Kong’s oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Campaigning begins for Japan's parliamentary election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Official campaigning for Japan's Oct. 27 parliamentary election began Tuesday with new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba seeking a mandate for his policies and for reforms after the governing party’s political funds scandal.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council expressed “strong concern” Monday as Israel has fired on and wounded U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon during intensified fighting, reiterating its support for their role in supporting security in the region. It's the first statement by the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Italy sends the first ship with migrants to Albania as rights groups warn of a dangerous precedent
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy is transferring the first group of migrants to Albania, the Interior Ministry said Monday, as part of a contentious plan to process thousands of asylum-seekers outside its borders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France’s far-right leader Marine Le Pen faces court on charges of embezzling EU funds
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen strongly denied committing any wrongdoing at a Paris court Monday as she and her National Rally party stand trial over the suspected embezzlement of European Parliament funds.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UNIFIL ignored Hezbollah terror build up for 18 years, Israel's UN ambassador says
item.description: Amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists, the U.N. is facing criticism that its peacekeeping force has failed to stop the Iran-terror proxy's military build up in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea sends warning to South Korea, saying troops ready to strike if more drones appear
item.description: North Korea said its army units are prepared to launch strikes against South Korea if another drone carrying propaganda flyers flies into the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran making billions in oil revenues under Biden admin as expert slams sanctions policy for lack of 'pressure'
item.description: Some critics have claimed the revenues do not paint an accurate picture due to the fluctuation of prices and lack of reliable sources, but an expert argued that other reports confirm an export surge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon will send advanced missile defense system, military personnel to Israel
item.description: The U.S. will send to Israel a missile defense system and military personnel to operate it, the Pentagon announced Sunday. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin authorized the deployment of a Terminal High-Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) battery "and associated crew of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, The Hill

item.title: Trump touts relationship with Kim Jong Un, Putin
item.description: Former President Trump on Sunday touted his relationships with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Indian politician with Bollywood ties shot dead in Mumbai weeks before key state election
item.description: Baba Siddique, 66, a senior politician in India’s financial capital Mumbai, has been fatally shot weeks before a key state election, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden: Israel should 'absolutely' stop strikes on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: President Biden said Friday that Israel should “absolutely” stop strikes on the U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon, following two incidents the group was involved in this week.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Navy SEALs' drownings in Iranian op were preventable, investigation finds
item.description: An investigation into the death of two Navy SEALS who drowned during a mission to seize Iranian missile parts bound for the Houthi rebels in Yemen found that the deaths could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mount Everest remains believed to be climber who vanished 100 years ago
item.description: A National Geographic documentary crew has found the possible partial remains of Andrew Irvine, a British climber who vanished on Mount Everest 100 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archaeologists unearth well-preserved 4,000-year-old Egyptian tomb
item.description: A joint Egyptian-German archaeological mission found the tomb of an ancient governor's daughter. Two tombs were found, one inside the other, covered in intricate script and art.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel continues to weigh options, timing on Iran strike following Biden-Netanyahu call
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a call that in part focused on Iran's recent missile attack on the Jewish state, and reportedly how Israel intends to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation
